pkg/defaultx.purpose: Tiny generic utility package providing nil-safe pointer dereferencing and zero-value fallback helpers. Used across domain packages to reduce nil guard boilerplate when handling optional/pointer fields.

patterns.name: WithDefault for pointer optionals
patterns.description: Use WithDefault[T](ptr, fallback) whenever a *T field may be nil and a non-pointer fallback value is needed. Never dereference pointers without this guard.

patterns.name: IfZero for zero-value replacement
patterns.description: Use IfZero[T](val, fallback) for comparable types where the zero value signals 'unset'. Do not use for pointer types — use WithDefault instead.

key_file_guides.role: Defines both exported functions; entire package API lives here.
key_file_guides.watch_for: IfZero uses comparable constraint — cannot be used with slices, maps, or non-comparable structs.

anti_patterns: Adding non-default-related utilities here — this package must stay focused on nil/zero fallbacks.
Using IfZero with pointer types (zero value of a pointer is nil, use WithDefault instead).

decisions.decision: Separate WithDefault (pointer) and IfZero (zero-value comparable) into two functions.
decisions.rationale: Pointer nil-check and zero-value check are distinct semantics; conflating them into one function would require type assertions or reflection.

pkg/entitydiff.purpose: Generic diffing library for entity collections that need create/update/delete reconciliation between expected state and persisted DB state. Used by billing subscription sync to reconcile invoice lines and subscription phases against their DB representations.

patterns.name: Entity interface constraint
patterns.description: All diffable types must implement Entity (GetID() string, IsDeleted() bool). ID-less items (GetID()=="") that are not deleted are always treated as creates.

patterns.name: DiffByIDEqualer for leaf entities
patterns.description: Use DiffByIDEqualer when the entity has no child entities and implements equal.Equaler[T]. It skips updates where PersistedState.Equal(ExpectedState) is true.

patterns.name: DiffByID with callbacks for parent entities
patterns.description: Use DiffByID with HandleCreate/HandleUpdate/HandleDelete callbacks when the entity has child entities that require their own nested diffing inside the update handler.
patterns.example: entitydiff.DiffByID(entitydiff.DiffByIDInput[Phase]{DBState: db, ExpectedState: exp, HandleUpdate: func(u DiffUpdate[Phase]) error { return diffChildren(u) }})

patterns.name: NestedEntity wrapper for parent context propagation
patterns.description: Wrap child entities in NestedEntity[T, P] or EqualerNestedEntity[T, P] when the diff handler needs access to the parent entity alongside the child during create/update/delete.

patterns.name: Diff.Append and Union for combining diffs
patterns.description: Use Diff.Append or Union[T] to merge diffs from multiple sub-trees before applying; never apply partial diffs and then compute a second diff on already-mutated state.

key_file_guides.role: Core diff logic: diffByID (internal), DiffByID (callback-based), DiffByIDEqualer (equality-based), Diff[T] struct with NeedsUpdate/NeedsCreate/NeedsDelete/Append/IsEmpty/Union.
key_file_guides.watch_for: diffByID treats items missing from expectedState as deletes — pass the full expected slice, not a filtered subset, or items will be spuriously deleted.

key_file_guides.role: NestedEntity and EqualerNestedEntity wrappers for carrying parent context into diff callbacks; NewEqualersWithParent helper.
key_file_guides.watch_for: EqualerNestedEntity.Equal delegates only to Entity.Equal — parent fields are NOT compared for equality, only used for context in callbacks.

anti_patterns: Implementing Entity.GetID() to return a non-stable ID (e.g. index-based) — IDs must be stable DB identifiers or empty string for new items.
Using DiffByID for leaf entities without children — use DiffByIDEqualer to skip no-op updates.
Mutating DB state inside a diff callback and then running another diff on the same collection — diffs are computed once against the original DB snapshot.
Passing a filtered expectedState slice to diffByID — items absent from expectedState are unconditionally deleted.

decisions.decision: Split diffByID (returns UpdateCandidates) from DiffByIDEqualer (filters candidates via Equal) into two separate code paths.
decisions.rationale: Entities with children cannot use equality comparison because child state is not embedded; callers with children need all update candidates to recurse into child diffs.

decisions.decision: IsDeleted() on the expected state drives deletion, not absence from the expected list alone.
decisions.rationale: Subscription sync needs to represent soft-delete intent explicitly (e.g. managedBy change co-located with deleted_at) so the deleted expected state carries the full target row to persist before deleting.

code_examples.scenario: Diff invoice lines (leaf entities with equality) and apply changes

pkg/equal.purpose: Minimal interface and helpers for structural equality comparison between domain types. Exists as a separate package to break circular dependencies when entitydiff and models both need the Equaler contract.

patterns.name: Equaler[T] interface
patterns.description: Implement Equal(other T) bool on value types that participate in entitydiff reconciliation or need pointer-safe equality. Do not implement on types that already embed a generated Equal from goderive.
patterns.example: func (l InvoiceLine) Equal(other InvoiceLine) bool { return l.ID == other.ID && l.Amount.Equal(other.Amount) }

patterns.name: PtrEqual for nullable Equaler fields
patterns.description: Use PtrEqual[T] when comparing two *T fields where both nil means equal. Avoids manual nil guard at call sites.

patterns.name: HasherPtrEqual for hash-comparable types
patterns.description: Use HasherPtrEqual[T] for types that implement hasher.Hasher instead of Equaler to compare by hash digest rather than field-by-field.

key_file_guides.role: Defines Equaler[T] interface and PtrEqual/HasherPtrEqual helpers. Entire package API.
key_file_guides.watch_for: HasherPtrEqual requires the type to implement pkg/hasher.Hasher — adding a new type here requires that dependency, not just equal.Equaler.

anti_patterns: Duplicating the Equaler interface in another package instead of importing pkg/equal — this breaks the entitydiff type constraint.
Implementing Equal with reflect.DeepEqual — breaks with pointer fields and is not safe for billing amount types.

decisions.decision: Equaler[T] lives in pkg/equal rather than pkg/models to avoid import cycles between entitydiff and models.
decisions.rationale: entitydiff imports equal; models imports entitydiff in some domains; a single shared equal package cuts the cycle.

pkg/errorsx.purpose: Error utility package providing prefix propagation through joined error trees, a warn-severity error wrapper, and the Handler/SlogHandler/NopHandler trio that implement httptransport.ErrorHandler for consistent error logging across HTTP transports.

patterns.name: WithPrefix for structured error wrapping
patterns.description: Use WithPrefix(err, prefix) instead of fmt.Errorf("%s: %w") when the error may be a joined error — it recursively prefixes each leaf error rather than wrapping the join.

patterns.name: NewWarnError for non-fatal errors
patterns.description: Wrap errors that should log as warnings (not errors) with NewWarnError before returning. SlogHandler detects *warnError and downgrades the log level.

patterns.name: SlogHandler as default HTTP error handler
patterns.description: Inject SlogHandler (not NopHandler) into httptransport.Handler via WithErrorHandler — it maps context.Canceled to Warn and *warnError to Warn automatically.

key_file_guides.role: WithPrefix recursive join-aware prefixer; warnError type and NewWarnError constructor.
key_file_guides.watch_for: WithPrefix only recurses one level via the Unwrap() []error interface check — errors wrapped with fmt.Errorf("%w", joinedErr) lose the recursive prefix on the inner errors.

key_file_guides.role: Handler interface (mirrors httptransport.ErrorHandler), SlogHandler, NopHandler. Used by HTTP transport layer.
key_file_guides.watch_for: SlogHandler.Handle and HandleContext differ only in context-aware vs non-context logging — always prefer HandleContext when a ctx is available.

anti_patterns: Using fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", prefix, joinedErr) instead of WithPrefix — loses per-leaf prefix propagation in joined errors.
Returning *warnError directly — always use NewWarnError which handles nil safely.
Using NopHandler in production HTTP handlers — silently swallows all transport errors.

decisions.decision: WithPrefix recurses through Unwrap() []error (joined errors) but stops at single-Unwrap wrappers.
decisions.rationale: Only top-level joins should be expanded; wrapping a join with fmt.Errorf is an intentional barrier that should not be silently penetrated.

pkg/expand.purpose: Generic type-safe expand/field-selection mechanism for API responses. Callers declare an Expandable[T] enum type; Expand[T] is a validated slice of those values with Has/With/Without/SetOrUnsetIf helpers used in service input types to control which nested relations are fetched.

patterns.name: Expandable enum type
patterns.description: Define a string-based enum type implementing Expandable[T] with a Values() []T method listing all valid expand keys. Use this as the type parameter for Expand[T] in service input structs.

patterns.name: Expand.Validate() in input validation
patterns.description: Call input.Expand.Validate() in service input Validate() methods to reject unknown expand values before passing them to the adapter.
patterns.example: if err := input.Expand.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, models.NewGenericValidationError(err) }

patterns.name: Expand.Has() in adapter queries
patterns.description: Use expand.Has(SomeExpandValue) inside adapter query builders to conditionally eager-load relations via Ent's .WithX() methods.
patterns.example: q := client.Invoice.Query(); if params.Expand.Has(InvoiceExpandLines) { q = q.WithLines() }

patterns.name: SetOrUnsetIf for conditional expand assembly
patterns.description: Use SetOrUnsetIf(condition, value) to build expand slices from boolean feature flags without manual append/filter logic.

key_file_guides.role: Full Expandable interface, Expand[T] type, and all methods. Entire package API.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Expandable requires comparable — string-based enums satisfy this; int-based enums do too, but struct-based expand keys would not compile.

anti_patterns: Passing raw string slices for expand fields instead of typed Expand[T] — loses compile-time safety and Validate().
Skipping Expand.Validate() in service input validation — allows unknown expand values to silently no-op in adapter queries.
Implementing Values() to return an empty slice — Validate() will reject all expand values including valid ones.

decisions.decision: Expand is a separate generic package rather than inlined per-domain.
decisions.rationale: Multiple domains (billing, subscription, customer) need the same validated expand-slice semantics; a shared package avoids duplicating the Has/With/Without logic.

code_examples.scenario: Define and use a typed expand for invoice queries
